title: Polypharmacy concern
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to clinician review of benefits, harms, adherence, interactions, monitoring and priorities without changing medicines.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
source.setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.

assessment.text: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to documenting values, preferences and questions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

assessment.text: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.
assessment.rationale: Relevant only when multimorbidity, function, frailty and medicine burden are actually assessed.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
source.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
source.setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

assessment.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to documenting treatment burden, daily life, carers, goals and priorities.
